headline: Judicial Appointments Commission has no public selection criteria
context: The Judicial Appointments Commission, established in 2009, still does not publish its criteria for recommending judicial candidates, operating entirely behind closed doors.

cards.big: Malaysia's judges are selected by a commission with no published criteria
cards.sub: JAC has operated for 17 years without disclosing how it evaluates candidates

cards.big: Article 122B gives PM final say after receiving JAC recommendations
cards.sub: Executive retains veto power over judicial selection despite reform commission

cards.big: UK's Judicial Appointments Commission publishes detailed competency frameworks
cards.sub: Transparent selection criteria enable public scrutiny and build judicial legitimacy

cards.big: JAC rejected only 4 PM nominees out of 340 since its 2009 establishment
cards.sub: 98.8% approval rate suggests commission defers to executive preference

cards.big: A selection commission that almost never disagrees with the PM is reform theatre
Should JAC publish its selection criteria and shortlists for superior court judges?
